title: Browser Origins, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Fields · Runtime Contract: Browser Security Headers
summary: A browser API reflects arbitrary Origin with credentials, mutates state on GET, accepts ambient cookies cross-site, caches preflight incorrectly, and treats CORS as server authorization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo browser security headers increment in browser request trust and cross-origin policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set content-type protection, framing, referrer, transport, content security, permissions, and cache policy according to whether responses are API data or HTML.
Select host-only scope, __Host or __Secure prefixes, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Partitioned, expiry, and rotation for the intended browser flow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Browser Security Headers · Theory Browser Security Headers
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1090 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to keep CORS response sharing separate from CSRF mutation authorization; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Browser Security Headers contract for the supplied data.
